videos.title: AFRICAN HEAD CHARGE @ Gaswerk Music Days | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
African Head Charge is a dub band from London, a long-running studio and live collaboration between master Jamaican percussionist Bonjo Iyabinghi Noah and English dub producer Adrian Sherwood, and a revolving cast of guest players. A unique blend of polyrhythmic outernational sounds.  A stylistic and conceptual similarity to Sherwood's own projects, such as Dub Syndicate, is no coincidence, although African Head Charge has a stronger psychedelic or "shamanistic" influence.

AFRICAN HEAD CHARGE @ Gaswerk Music Days Festival, Berlin | 2024
A series by @berta.berlin and ŌĆ¬@GaswerkMusicDaysŌĆ¼

videos.title: TYSHAWN SOREY TRIO live @ Stadtgarten, K├Čln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2025
videos.description:
"Tyshawn Sorey is dedicated to ŌĆśreformulating perceptions of modern Black/Afrodiasporic creative practice.ŌĆÖ With roots in the complexities of avant-garde classical, he was honoured with the 2024 Pulitzer Prize for Music for his beautiful, understated Adagio (For Wadada Leo Smith.
Sorey also has, he says, ŌĆśa lifelong connection to the ŌĆśstraight-aheadŌĆÖ and the Great American Songbook which he shares with Diehl, with whom he has collaborated over many years, akin to ŌĆśa brotherly connectionŌĆÖ. Raghaven, highly respected and in-demand, has recently joined the trio.
A remarkable dialogue of exploration that flipped between pure abstract impressionism and an obliquely sidelong visit to the universe of mainstream jazz, setting invention and re-invention side by side." (Geoff Winston for UKJazzNews)

videos.title: Ingebrigt H├źker Flaten (Exit) Knarr @ Concert Hall of HfMT | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2025
videos.description:
The origins of (Exit) Knarr reveal a spark of creative synergy that has only grown stronger with time. For those familiar with H├źker FlatenŌĆÖs three-decade career as one of the finest musicians in jazz and improvised music, it was clear from the start that this project was something special. (Exit) Knarr is not just a band but a space for artistic collaboration, where great musicians come together to elevate one another and create something truly unique. This ethos is evident in the sheer musical joy captured on Drops. Few bands can match the vibrant energy and sense of discovery that (Exit) Knarr embodies, making it not just a thrill to hear but undoubtedly a joy to be part of. The band transforms abstract concepts into visceral, shape-shifting soundscapes rooted in jazz, improvisation, and electronic music.

(Exit) Knarr - live @ Concert Hall of HfMT, K├Čln | 2025
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by @berta.berlin and @colognejazzweek

videos.title: DUST BUNNY - live @ JAKI, K├Čln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2025
videos.description:
In German, ŌĆ£Dust BunnyŌĆØ is a wool mouse. The name given to balls of organic and inorganic material that are often found in hard-to-reach corners of a home. ŌĆ£Dust BunnyŌĆØ is also the title of a horror film and the name of a character in a computer game. One might almost say that it is the pop music sidekick of the Berlin-based Polish artist Marta Warelis and drummer Nasim Lopez-Palcios Navarro ŌĆō if it werenŌĆÖt for her anarchic joy in sweeping away beautiful sounds and melodies as soon as they appear in the performance. The two play ritualistic music with which they impetuously attempt to push contrasts to their limits. Warelis on synthesizer and keyboards and Navarro on drums play across the dividing lines of prog rock and noise, ambient and free jazz as if it were the most natural thing in the world, celebrating a wild rave that is loud, hypnotic, and iridescent all at once.

DUST BUNNY - live @ JAKI, K├Čln | 2025
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by ŌĆ¬@berta.berlinŌĆ¼ &ŌĆ¬ ŌĆ¬@colognejazzweekŌĆ¼

videos.description:
It takes a lot for a double bass player to record his debut album as an unaccompanied solo improvisation. His debut ŌĆśNullŌĆÖ (ŌĆśZeroŌĆÖ) contains all the musical, aesthetic and technical qualities that have made Landfermann an internationally sought-after soloist ever since. You can sense his sense of adventure when he ventures into unknown territory. And yes, you also experience something of LandfermannŌĆÖs first musical love: when the sixteenth-note chains clack furiously, his solo excursions also have something of heavy metal about them.

videos.title: Voutchkova & Amadou @ DARA String Festival - Theater im Delphi | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
DARA String Festival presents a wide range of innovative, genre-bending music for string instruments, ranging from early contemporary music to experimental
and freely improvised music. DARA transcends genre boundaries, bringing together high-caliber international artists from diverse musical backgrounds in a series of encounters to stimulate new thoughts and ideas for the advancement of individual and collective artistic endeavors.

Funded by Musikfonds e.V. by means of the Federal Government Commissioner for Culture and the Media, and INM Berlin. 
Gef├Črdert vom Musikfonds e.V. mit Projektmitteln der Beauftragten der Bundesregierung f├╝r Kultur und Medien, und INM Berlin. 

Biliana Voutchkova & Farida Amadou - live @ Theater im Delphi, Berlin | 2023
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer:
Farida Amadou - bass guitar
Biliana Voutchkova - violin, voice

Biliana Voutchkova
She is a dynamic, thoroughly engaged interdisciplinary artist, violinist, composer-performer, improvisor and curator with highly individual, unconventional artistic language. Her work spans the widest possible range of sound, vision, movement and includes concert performances of contemporary composition and original site specific work, exhibitions, long durational / multidisciplinary performances. She is based between Berlin and her rural residency on the black sea coast of Bulgaria.
http://www.bilianavoutchkova.net/

Farida Amadou
Farida is a self taught bass player based in Brussels, Belgium. The electric bass is her main instrument since 2011. She is focused on her solo improvisation practice and collaborations with musicians like Steve Noble, Thurston Moore, Peter Br├Čtzmann, Floris Vanhoof, Julien Desprez among other occasional featuring such as Jerusalem in My Heart and Moor Mother.
https://faridamadou.com/

videos.title: Andromeda Mega Express Orchestra - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | KOSMOSTAGE IV
videos.description:
Celebrating its 15th anniversary this year, Berlin-based Andromeda Mega Express Orchestra is opening the festival Kosmostage IV with a program by ensemble leader/composer Daniel Glatzel. Looking within, the first two titles are impressions which are formally structured and orchestrated but each time collectively shaped anew in swarm-like and fluid movements. The 18-minute ŌĆ£CurrentsŌĆØ flows through complex swaying rhythms steadily upstream, whatever may come, on and on.

videos.title: Nils Wogram "Muse" - live @ Sendesaal, Bremen | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
Nils Wogram's music has always had a great deal of sensuality, and in this project that means all the more: All or nothing! Sound is the key component. Trombone, saxophone, harp, viola and voice are the basis for this. The visionary, Nils Wogram, who never just searches for the sake of searching, but who makes the impossible possible and the unheard of, without any compromise.
The essence is the great sensitivity with which the musicians listen to each other, how they react and interact to the sounds they create together. 
This music is lyrical and full of poetry. Some kind kind of improvised chamber music with an affinity to film, contemporary classical, folk and jazz.
Deutscher Jazzpreis/German Jazz-Award 2022 - Album Instrumental

Nils Wogram "Muse" - live @ Sendesaal, Bremen | 2021
Filmed By Ear is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: FRED MOTEN & BRANDON LOPEZ ŌĆō Perspectives | Part 1 | Konzerthaus Berlin
videos.description:
Fred Moten and Brandon L├│pez make incendiary music for a burning world. Their infamous live 
shows explore a propulsive, relentless, and energetic large-scale developing form. Poet and cultural 
theorist Fred Moten creates new conceptual spaces that accommodate emergent forms of Black cultural production, aesthetics, and social life. The composer  and bassist Brandon L├│pez engages with work,  improvisation, and finding new modalities and sonic possibilities on the double bass. 
As a duo, they concern themselves with the necessity for improvisation of navigating the ascending reign of long-institutionalized madness while simultaneously keeping humanity and sanity intact. In 2025, their synergistic mesh in duo was presented on record for the first time on Revision. 

FRED MOTEN & BRANDON LOPEZ live @ Konzerthaus, Berlin | 2026
STOP OVER 4 ŌĆō Perspectives (Part 1)

The fourth stopover on the path to establishing the ŌĆ×ZentrumŌĆ£ focused on curation: Three internationally renowned musicians have been invited to shape one evening each within this three-day series. videos.title: DAVIES MARINO WALKER ŌĆō Perspectives | Part 1 | Konzerthaus Berlin
videos.description:
Davies-Marino-Walker is a new trio grounded in a shared commitment to active listening, experimentation, and expanding the sonic possibilities of their instruments. Angharad Davies brings her unique vocabulary of alternative resonating bodies, while Jessie Marino infuses abstract ideas drawn from popular culture and the everyday. Deborah Walker, in turn, explores the intersection between music and other art forms.
Folk traditions have long influenced their individual practices, leading them to explore the interconnectedness of music and the art of weaving, a vital craft found worldwide that has remained largely unchanged throughout history. Weaved objects shape everyday life in often overlooked yet profound ways. The trioŌĆÖs Ecstatic Looms pieces, inspired by historic weaving
techniques, use notation from traditional pattern books as a springboard for improvised musical structures and materials, weaving together sound and textureŌĆöalways welcoming accidental discoveries along the way.

DAVIES MARINO WALKER live @ Konzerthaus, Berlin | 2026

videos.title: SATISFACTIONACTION @ Deutsche Oper, Berlin | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
In SATISFACTIONACTION the gratification is both felt on an individual level and celebrated communally, with the public immersed in a multisensory experience. It also poses the question: can a transparently escapist, communal moment counterbalance a permanent feeling of crisis and pressure? Does communal satisfaction provide the basis for agreeing on a new understanding of reality, or is it just us running away from a world on fire?

The contemporary music of composer Max Andrzejewski is informed by the minimal and jazz genres and the ASMR movement. As a percussionist with improvised-music leanings, he is also one of the eight members of the SATISFACTIONACTION ensemble, which is made up of Kaleidoskop soloists and other musicians with close artistic links to Andrzejewski. This collaboration with the singers of the Vocantare Chorus and choreographer Sylvana Seddig is embedded within the space and video installation of Lukas Zerbst. The result is a composition consisting of spatially and temporally shifting and overlapping layers, merging into constellations, one that mirrors and renders experiential the bases of our need for fulfillment.

Impressions of the installation music theatre piece SATISFACTIONACTION by Max Andrzejewski - premiered in 2025. A co-production by Deutsche Oper Berlin, Beethovenfest Bonn and Max Andrzejewski - supported by Hauptstadtkulturfonds.

SATISFACTIONACTION @ Deutsche Oper, Berlin | 2025
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: K├®k Ker├®k by F. Sn├®tberger, Ferenc Sn├®tberger, guitar solo
videos.description:
www.snetberger.com, www.ecmrecords.com
Born in 1957 in northern Hungary, guitarist/composer Ferenc Sn├®tberger was the youngest son of a family of musicians. Early on his father, a guitar player also, became his role model. Sn├®tberger studied classical music and jazz guitar. Today he is best known for his art of improvising and his crossing of stylistic borders. His music is inspired by the Roma tradition of his home country, Brazilian music and flamenco as well as classical guitar playing and jazz. He made numerous albums as a leader, co-leader and sideman and has toured all over Europe as well as Japan, Korea, India and the United States.

videos.title: Linda May Han Oh: Aventurine | JAZZ NIGHT IN AMERICA
videos.description:
As a bassist and bandleader, Linda May Han Oh has demonstrated her gift for liquid dynamism, not only within her peer group but also with heroic elders like guitarist Pat Metheny. As a composer and arranger, Oh recently scaled up her ambitions with Aventurine, a sparkling album featuring what you might call a double quartet, though the music tells a more seamless story.

Jazz Night in America teamed up with CapitalBop's Traveling Loft Series to present this project at NPR's Studio One in Washington, D.C. Just as she did on the album, Oh led a chamber-jazz ensemble whose "jazz" side consists of Greg Ward on saxophones, Matt Mitchell on piano and Ches Smith on drums and vibraphone. On the "classical" side were violinists Fung Chern Hwei and Curtis Stewart, violist Benni von Gutzeit and cellist Jeremy Harman. The cohesion among these musicians ran deep throughout the concert, which captures a serious talent pushing the limits ŌĆö and not just her own.

This concert was recorded at NPR's Studio One in Washington, D.C., on July 1, 2019.

To learn more about Linda May Han Oh, check out our radio episode delving into Oh's unique background and broadminded musical intentions: https://www.npr.org/2020/03/11/814612538/bassist-linda-may-han-oh-is-a-musician-rooted-in-curiosity

SET LIST
00:00 - "Kirigami"
10:44 - "Song Yue Rao (Moon in the Pines)"

videos.title: Magro - Trippin (feat. TwizzMatic & Kurt Rosenwinkel)
videos.description:
"Trippin" is the first single from Magro`s album of the same name, which will be released on May 28th. On this track he features US rapper TwizzMatic and guitar legend Kurt Rosenwinkel.
It is an experimental mix of Jazz and Hip Hop and describes the ambiguity and naive immaturity of our society today. After having worked with numerous artists as a drummer and producer over the years (including Leona Berlin, Kenny Wesley, J Lamotta, Stimulus), Magro is now releasing his first album under his own name.

videos.title: LIBELLE @ Jazzwerkstatt Peitz | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
With LIBELLE, Slavin succeeds in striking a balance between structure and openness, between subtle grooves, dense textures, and spontaneous interaction. Each concert is an organic process ŌĆō sonically versatile, rhythmically precise, and shaped by a deep sensitivity to dynamics and atmosphere. Improvisation here is not a decorative element, but the beating heart of the music.

This concert took place as part of the Jazz Prize Brandenburg 2025 award ceremony, at which Wanja Slavin was honored.
Supported by Initiative Musik gGmbH with project funds from the Federal Government Commissioner for Culture and the Media.

videos.title: G├╝nter "Baby" Sommer & Percussion Staff - live @ Festungsturm | JAZZWERKSTATT PEITZ
videos.description:
This extraordinary percussion ensemble around Francesco D'Auria and G├╝nter "Baby" Sommer have been around for 35 years: The magical world of percussion, the ongoing search for new sounds from the instruments, the somnambulistic connection between the musicians - all this makes Percussion Staff so unique. The song of the elements of drum skins, wood, metal, water is joined by the melodic sound of marimba and vibraphone, trumpet and flugelhorn, whistle and shell and human voice, combine to form music that smells of the sea and olives, in which one can Hear the buzzing of the crickets.

videos.title: Peter Zegveld & Terrie Ex - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival
videos.description:
When the pot boils over, the feedback wails and the tea kettle whistles, itŌĆÖs clear: Peter Zegveld and Terrie Ex are about to shock your system. The Dutch duo transform former industrial production lines into an adventurous audio playground. Musical craftsmanship becomes post-workerist expressionism. While performance artist Zegveld toils in his sound machine theatre like a deranged puppeteer, Terrie Hessels takes all of the power of deformation and the art of distortion that earned him the honorific Terrie Ex during his many years with The Ex and unleashes it full force on the guitar. Jazz that you can really let off steam to ŌĆō does that even still exist? It certainly does. And, with Zegveld & Ex, you can take that literally.

Peter Zegveld & Terrie Ex - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | 2021
A series by @berta.berlin and A L'ARME! Festival.
Watch all concert films here: https://www.alarmefestival.de/concert-films-alarme

videos.title: Alva Noto 'Hadron Prototype' A LŌĆÖARME! Festival 2020
videos.description:
alva noto . Hadron Prototype . A LŌĆÖARME! Festival 2020


[EN] Alva Noto presents the world premiere of his new work HADRON PROTOTYPE. Electronic sound and radioactivity are stripped back to their most elemental in a new audio-visual performance. Alva Noto is a minimalist in the truest sense of the word, reducing electronic music to its mathematical fundamentals, delving into the bewildering cloud of information that makes up the computerized sound. He leads us into this stormy digital landscape, where glitchy electronic rumblings and synth thunderclaps cut through a shower of white noise. This environment is echoed in his accompanying video work, which turns scientific phenomena ŌĆō like sound and light frequencies ŌĆō into a hallucinatory video experience.
 
[DE] Carsten Nicolai geht es seit jeher um die Kraft des Elementaren. Unter seinem Alias Alva Noto pr├żsentiert der Musiker und bildende K├╝nstler bei A L'ARME! die Weltpremiere von ŌĆ×HADRON PROTOTYPEŌĆ£: eine audiovisuelle Performance, in der Radioaktivit├żt und elektronische Musik auf ihre Urspr├╝nge zur├╝ckf├╝hrt werden. Nicolai ist ein Minimalist im wahrsten Sinne des Wortes. Er durchdringt die verwirrende Informationsdichte, aus der Computerkl├żnge gemacht scheinen und reduziert sie auf ihre mathematischen Grundlagen ŌĆō so glasklar wie elegant. Mit ŌĆ×HADRON PROTOTYPEŌĆ£ entf├╝hrt er das Publikum in digitale Landschaften, in der das Knistern von Rechenprozessen und der Donnerschlag der Synthesizer vor dem gesamten Frequenzspektrum widerhallen. Die Klangkulisse findet ihre Entsprechung in einer gro├¤en Video- und LED-Installation, auf der Nicolai die physikalischen Prozesse hinter Licht und Ton in ein halluzinatorisches Videoerlebnis verwandelt.

Die Lieder handeln von Liebe und Sehnsucht, Widerstand, Opfern und Held_innen. Lurische Ges├żnge sind der Inbegriff m├╝ndlich ├╝berlieferter Geschichte, Oral History. Sie sind reich an Metaphern und dr├╝cken eine besondere Verbundenheit zu Natur in den lurischen Provinzen aus. Das Wort Ilyad ist eine Zusammensetzung aus Il ŌĆō Stamm der durch Zagros zieht und Yad ŌĆō die Erinnerung daran.

Metal Theatre: Aftermath
Die Band zelebriert die Musik von Nikan Khosravi, dem Frontmann der Metalband Confess, in einer einzigartigen und frenetischen Umgebung. Die Musiker_innen bringen gemeinsame Erfahrungen von Aufruhr, Widerstand und Poetik aus der Asche auf die B├╝hne. In der ersten gemeinsamen Zusammenarbeit teilen sie gemeinsame Wahrnehmungen von Ereignissen in ihrer unmittelbaren Umgebung und wie sie zu globalen Fragen stehen. Carsta K├Čhler verwandelt die Performer_innen in ihren Kost├╝men in wahrhaft glorreiche B├╝hnenfiguren. Ein Konzert mit einpr├żgsamen Riffs, intensiven Improvisationen, dichten Ger├żuscherlebnissen und Stimmen von Fl├╝stern bis zu Schreien. F├╝r alle, die Freude haben an den zwingenden Kr├żften von Klang.

videos.title: Klangteppich V. - Loom
videos.description:
#loom #soundart #weaving

Loom
F├żden sind ein Thema, das Klangteppich immer wieder durchzieht. In Loom kreieren der Klangk├╝nstler Iman Jesmi und die Weberin Kira de Picciotto mit ihrem Webstuhl eine Performance ├╝ber die Ber├╝hrungen zwischen Tastsinn und Textil. Die Zeitlichkeit von Kl├żngen erh├żlt mit der Fertigung des Textils eine Stofflichkeit, die im Verlauf sichtbar wird. Der sonst verborgene Prozess des Webens tritt auf der B├╝hne in Erscheinung, mit feinen Handbewegungen und notwendigen Mechanismen mit einem eigenen Spektrum an Kl├żngen. Was bedeuten die Ber├╝hrung einer sichtlichen Oberfl├żche und das Muster auf ihrer Kehrseite als Abbild des umgesetzten Werks? Die Auff├╝hrung sensibilisiert die Empfindungen von akustischen und taktilen Erlebnissen.

videos.description:
Dard I Door ŌĆō Ein ferner Schmerz live beim Klangteppich IV Festival 2022 mit Tanasgol Sabbagh - Spoken Word und Reza Askari - Kontrabass.

Der Kontrabassist Reza Askari n├żhert sich gemeinsam mit der Spoken-Word-K├╝nstlerin Tanasgol Sabbagh der komplexen und auch widerspr├╝chlichen Pers├Čnlichkeit seines Vaters. Mohammad Reza Askari-Motlagh studierte in den 1960er Jahren in Deutschland, als Arzt sorgte er in den 1980er Jahren f├╝r die Einreise und Behandlung von Kindern aus dem Iran-Irak-Krieg nach Deutschland. Sabbagh schreibt Gedichte und performative Texte, in denen sie gesellschaftliche Missst├żnde aus pers├Čnlicher Perspektive aufgreift. Die beiden K├╝nstler_innen loten aus, inwiefern sich ihre Erfahrungen ├żhneln, ihr Selbstverst├żndnis und ihre Haltung zu gesellschaftlichen Themen gepr├żgt haben. Ein Schl├╝ssel daf├╝r ist der Rhythmus, der das Instrument Kontrabass und die Spoken-Word-Performance verbindet.

videos.description:
Wired live at Klangteppich IV Festival 2022 with Azin Zahedi - Nazanin Noori - Farahnaz Hatam - Shabnam Parvaresh and installation by Haleh Redjaian.

With her geometric installation of threads at Theater Aufbau Kreuzberg, artist Haleh Redjaian creates a space and stage of her own for the encounter between musicians of acoustic and electronic instruments. The ensemble takes telegraphy, the central communication technology of the 19th century, as an occasion to explore the relationships between sound, interaction and space, between noise, breath and resonances. One artist is not physically present, but all the more audible.

videos.title: Liza ŌĆō a liminal journey - Shaahin Peymani - Judith Hamann - Florian Bergmann | Klangteppich IV
videos.description:
Liza ŌĆō a liminal journey - Shaahin Peymani - sound art, Judith Hamann ŌĆō cello, Florian Bergmann ŌĆō  bass clarinet.

Sound artist Shaahin Peymani is the grandson of Elisabeth Agnes Preis. More than fifty years ago, she decided to live in Iran. In August 1968, she and her husband Seyed Kazem Hanafi Yazdi and their eight-year-old son Thomas traveled by car from Wuppertal via St. Lorenz and Graz in Austria, Zagreb and Belgrade in Yugoslavia, Sofia in Bulgaria, Istanbul, Ankara, Samsun and Erzurum in Turkey via Tabriz and Karaj to Tehran. 
Peymani found her travel diary, in which she describes the route, places of stay, encounters with long-distance drivers, "guest workers" and globetrotters, meals and German songs sung on the journey. His grandmother's journey is the basis for an electroacoustic work that Peymani performed for the first time at Klangteppich IV together with cellist Judith Hamann and clarinetist Florian Bergmann.

Recordet at tak Theater Aufbau Kreuzberg by berta.berlin  - sound recording Torben Utecht - photos by Eli Cornejo und Peter Ohnacker.

videos.title: FREQ.EXP by Arshia Samsaminia with Johannes Fink and Nikolaus Schlierf | Klangteppich IV 2022
videos.description:
The Tehran-based composer Arshia Samsaminia composed an electroacoustic work for the cellist and bass player Johannes Fink and the viola and violin player Nikolaus Schlierf that was premiered at Klangteppich IV in June 2022. Samsaminia deals with questions of transferring the specific tunings and intervals of Persian music into notation systems of European music and into software used in compositions with electronics. In ŌĆ×Freq.Exp.ŌĆ£, Fink and Schlierf gradually change instruments from double bass to cello, from violin to viola.
Johannes Fink is a sought-after musician in ensembles of improvised music, for example with pianist Aki Takase or saxophonist Daniel Erdmann.

Nikolaus Schlierf plays in ensembles of contemporary music and baroque music, he is a founding member of the Sonar Quartet and a regular guest of the ensembles Lux:NM and Adapter. Both musicians compose for ensembles in which they also perform, Fink for his own trio and Schlierf for the Sonar Quartet.

videos.title: RCO Berlin/Athens ŌĆō Electronic Sound Experiment  | KLANGTEPPICH III Festival 2021
videos.description:
Die Auff├╝hrung bei Klangteppich III haben RCO live ├╝ber die Entfernung mit ~ in Berlin und Karl Heinz Jeron in Athen realisiert.

G├żste* vom Noiselab-Festival

RCO entwirft elektronische Schaltungen und verdrahtet Alltagsgegenst├żnde, um Kl├żnge zu erzeugen und mit selbstgenerierten Ger├żuschen zu experimentieren. Unterschiedliche Ger├żtschaften, akustische Quellen und Herzschrittmacher werden in Feedbackschleifen und ├£berlagerungen zusammengebracht und in vielschichtigen Klanginstallationen arrangiert.
Recorded at tak Theater Aufbau Kreuzberg by berta.berlin

~
Der Konzeptk├╝nstler lebt und arbeitet in Berlin, Leipzig und Athen. Seine k├╝nstlerische T├żtigkeit ist prozessorientiert, performativ, poetisch und meist seriell. Schwerpunkte bilden dabei Eingriffe in bestehende Systeme sowie Konstruktionen sozialer R├żume und interaktiver Ereignisse. In seiner aktuellen Arbeit legt der K├╝nstler seinen Namen ab.

Karl Heinz Jeron wurde 1962 in Memmingen, Deutschland geboren. Er lebt und arbeitet in Athen und Berlin. Seine Kunst handelt von Alltagskultur, medialer Wahrnehmung und Informationsverarbeitung. Das reicht von Auff├╝hrungen mit singenden und tanzenden Robotern, ├╝ber Kompositionen mit elektronisch verschaltetem Gem├╝se bis hin zu Audio-Touren in Windparks. Seine Arbeiten wurden unter anderem im ZKM Karlsruhe, auf der Ars Electronica Linz, der Documenta X, am ICA London, dem Walker Art Museum Minneapolis, der Berlinischen Galerie Berlin und dem Museum of Modern Art San Francisco gezeigt.

videos.title: Hadi Bastani + Yalda Zamani - Electronic Music Performance | KLANGTEPPICH III Festival 2021
videos.description:
Die Zusammenarbeit des Komponisten, Klangk├╝nstlers und Anthropologen Hadi Bastani mit der Dirigentin, Forscherin und Performerin Yalda Zamani ist in mehrfacher Hinsicht eine Premiere: W├żhrend seines ersten Aufenthalts in Berlin kreierte Bastani eine Performance mit Zamani, in der sie zum ersten Mal mit Live-Coding und der Anwendung von Machine-Learning-Modellen in Kombination mit Motion-Sensing-Technologien arbeitet.

Berlin
Hadi Bastani [artist in residence] ŌĆō modular synthesizer
Yalda Zamani ŌĆō electronic sounds, codes, motion-sensors
Recorded at tak Theater Aufbau Kreuzberg by berta.berlin

Bastani arbeitet mit Field Recordings, er kreiert ortsspezifische, immersive und interaktive Installationen. Er performt mit Laptop, analogen Modularsynthesizern und gehackten elektronischen Spielzeugen.
In seinen k├╝nstlerischen, forschenden und lehrenden T├żtigkeiten widmet Bastani sich der Beziehung zwischen kreativer Praxis, digitalen Technologien, Improvisation und Entstehen neuer Formen von Gemeinschaften. Besonderes Augenmerk legt er auf die M├Čglichkeiten gemeinschaftlich entwickelter ethnographischer und kompositorischer Verfahren und Produktionen von Klangkunst unabh├żngig von den jeweiligen Standorten der Akteure.

Yalda Zamani ist eine in Deutschland lebende Dirigentin, Forscherin und Performerin, die sich f├╝r
transdisziplin├żre Ans├żtze in der zeitgen├Čssischen Musikperformance, Computerkreativit├żt
Live-Codierung und der Anwendung maschineller Lernmodelle in Kombination mit Technologien f├╝r Bewegungssensorik interessiert. Sie untersucht Interaktionen zwischen Menschen und KI-Systemen bei der Kreation von Musik, um die Beziehung einer Dirigentin, eines Dirigenten mit dem Orchester/Ensemble in der Auff├╝hrung neu zu definieren. Au├¤erdem umfasst ihre Forschung, wie die Entwicklung intelligenter k├╝nstlerischer ├¢kosysteme mit sinnstiftender Zusammenarbeit und heutigen Werten vereinbart werden kann.
2021 erhielt sie ein zweij├żhriges Doktorand_innen-Forschungsstipendium der Stadt Hamburg und des Europ├żischen Sozialfonds, das darauf abzielt, den Anteil von Frauen in F├╝hrungspositionen effektiv und nachhaltig zu erh├Čhen.

Eine Nacht, in der Menschen die Stadien zwischen Wachzustand, Halbschlaf, Schlaf und Aufwachen miteinander verbringen. Das Publikum nimmt auf Matratzen und Kissen Platz, die Performer_innen geleiten die Besucher_innen ├╝ber die Schwelle zur Entspannung, Nachtruhe und Tr├żumen, bis zum Morgengrauen und zur R├╝ckkehr bewusster Sinnesempfindungen. Die Performer_innen rezitieren Lyrik auf Persisch, Deutsch, Franz├Čsisch, Englisch und Chinesisch, au├¤erdem erklingen gesungene und produzierte, klassische persische Schlaflieder und popul├żre Songs. Der Klangk├╝nstler und Komponist Hadi Bastani komponiert die Klangatmosph├żren aus Ambient und Drone Music, die dazu einladen, sich ganz auf den Schauplatz einzulassen. Pariyestan ist die Interpretation eines mystischen M├żrchenwaldes, inspiriert von aktivistischen Bewegungen, die Ruhe als Widerstand und Resilienz f├╝r nicht-wei├¤e, queere und trans* Menschen in den Vordergrund r├╝cken. Die Performance tritt ein f├╝r Langsamkeit, Antiproduktivit├żt, F├╝rsorge und Teilhabe f├╝r alle, insbesondere f├╝r diejenigen, die von unzeitgem├ż├¤er Gesetzgebung traumatisiert und benachteiligt sind.

Aufgezeichnet im tak Theater Aufbau Kreuzberg von berta.berlin  - Tonmeister Torben Utecht und Alex Hollnack, Lichttechniker Stefan Ionita.

Premiere bei Klangteppich. Festival for music of the Iranian diaspora IV.
https://www.klangteppich.berlin/

#pariyestan #restingisresisting #antiproductivity #slowness #poetry #community

videos.title: Pheromone Poetry "Lacerta Cluster" - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | KOSMOSTAGE IV
videos.description:
Led by musical pheromones, the path that leads through ŌĆ£Lacerta ClusterŌĆØ is quite intricate and convoluted: Beginning with a hymn dedicated to our skeleton, itŌĆÖs followed by a friendship song, the sound of three flutes ŌĆō one of which copulates with a clarinet ŌĆō and a noble composition that beguiles the note D. Moving on, it leads past the erotic allure of a saxophone wrestling with the New York SchoolŌĆÖs enthusiastic lack of intention, to finally culminate in a levitating free jazz quartet.

videos.title: AKI TAKASE | JAPANIC - Monday in Budapest
videos.description:
Featuring:
    Aki Takase - piano
    Daniel Erdmann - saxophone
    DJ Illvibe - turntables, electronics
    Johannes Fink - double bass
    Dag Magnus Narvesen - drums

Pianist Aki Takase, born in Japan and living in Berlin since 1987, is one of the most outstanding and formative figures in contemporary jazz. Having already become a legend of jazz music, Aki TakaseŌĆÖs art is always dedicated to new projects and musicians who have a similarly open understanding of music as herself. In previous projects she has made eye-catching excursions to the boundaries between jazz and new music, dance and electronic music. Among her numerous projects the study of personalities in American jazz history was always a central issue, including giants as Duke Ellington (1990, 2009), Thelonious Monk (1994), Eric Dolphy (1998), W.C. Handy (2002), Fats Waller (2004) and Ornette Coleman (2006). The visitors of the Opus Jazz Club could listen to her marvellous Duke Ellington solo program in the beginning of last year.

In 2018 Aki Takase, celebrating her 70th birthday this year, is planning ŌĆō for the first time in quite some time ŌĆō a project that contains only pieces composed by her. Aki Takase draws on an international and young, but highly honored and world-famous generation of musicians: Echo winner German tenor saxophone player Daniel Erdmann (Velvet Revolution, das Kapital), his compatriots DJ Illvibe (SEEED, LycheeLassi, Peter Fox) and bass player Johannes Fink (Rolf K├╝hn Trio) and the Norwegian drummer Dag Magnus Narvesen (Splitter Orchestra) are among the elite of contemporary jazz, bringing with them very different styles and timbres. The combination of different generations, nationalities and musical backgrounds fits in perfectly with Aki Takase's overarching musical approach, which uses all fields of music history and has already earned her a firm place in the history of jazz over her more than 30 years of music activity.

The quintet are in residence at BMC the week preceding the concert, working out the project to its final form and recording it for an album to be released on BMC Records.

Sponsored by
Initiative Musik gGmbH
www.initiative-musik.de

Julia H├╝lsmann Quartet - Not Far From Here

Listen to or buy the album here: https://ecm.lnk.to/NotFarFromHereID

Uli Kempendorff: tenor saxophone
Julia H├╝lsmann: piano
Marc Muellbauer: double bass
Heinrich K├Čbberling: drums

ECM 2664

Not Far From Here introduces award-winning pianist Julia H├╝lsmannŌĆÖs new group in which her long-time working trio with Marc Muellbauer and Heinrich K├Čbberling (together now for 17 years) is expanded to a quartet with the addition of Berlin-based saxophonist Uli Kempendorff. In KempendorffŌĆÖs playing ŌĆ£both suppleness and unruliness are in ample supplyŌĆØ, Jazzthetik has noted, and he brings a fresh energy to the H├╝lsmann group concept. Each member of the quartet brings in original compositions, with Julia herself contributing five pieces for the album.  Repertoire is augmented by a timely cover of ŌĆ£This Is Not AmericaŌĆØ, David BowieŌĆÖs 1980s hit single (co-composed by Bowie, Pat Metheny and Lyle Mays), which appears here in a quartet version and a solo piano variation.  The album, recorded at Studios La Buissonne in the South of France in March 2019 is issued as the H├╝lsmann Quartet embarks on an extensive European tour. Dates include ECM50 events at the London Jazz Festival and Le Flagey, Brussels.

videos.title: Florian RossŌĆÖ Architexture ŌĆō Works for Jazz Quartet and Wind Ensemble (Full Trailer)
videos.description:
Described as a ŌĆśmusical explorerŌĆÖ, Florian Ross effortlessly spans the realms of improvised and composed jazz in a dazzling variety of formats, with music that comes from a deep synthesis of heart and mind. Architexture is RossŌĆÖs response in music to architects and buildings all over the world. From sumptuous Rococo decoration, to the ŌĆśGarage HousesŌĆÖ of US suburbia, and from the playful/serious style of Gaud├Ł to the modern utopia of NiemeyerŌĆÖs Brasilia ŌĆō Architexture weighs light and shade, symmetry and asymmetry in a unique fusion of rhythm, colour and lyrical invention.

Soli spielen  Johan H├Črl├®n am Altsaxofon und Chefdirigent Bob Mintzer am Tenorsaxofon. Gast an den Perkussions ist Bodek Janke.

Einem breiteren Publikum d├╝rfte Bill Laurance als Gr├╝ndungsmitglied und langj├żhriger Keyboarder des international erfolgreichen Kollektivs Snarky Puppy ein Begriff sein. Laurance ist ein sehr vielseitiger Musiker, der ├╝ber eine kaum ├╝berschaubare musikalische Bandbreite verf├╝gt. Im Herbst 2018 traf der britische Musiker zum ersten Mal auf die WDR BIG BAND, die Zusammenarbeit m├╝ndete in einem gemeinsamen Konzert in der K├Člner Philharmonie.

videos.title: Shake Stew - I Can Feel The Heat Closing In (Live Video)
videos.description:
Live-Performance of "I Can Feel The Heat Closing In" from our show at Porgy & Bess in November 2019. This track is part of our new album (A)live! which will be available from September 18, 2020 on Limited Edition Black 180g Vinyl including a deluxe 20-page Booklet, CD and Download/Stream.

Order the album on http://www.shakestew.bandcamp.com
For all live dates please visit http://www.shakestew.com

Lukas Kranzelbinder - Bass, Leader
Clemens Salesny - Alto Sax
Mario Rom - Trumpet
Johannes Schleiermacher - Tenor Sax
Oliver Potratz - Bass
Mathias Koch - Drums
Niki Dolp - Drums

"I Can Feel The Heat Closing In" written by Lukas Kranzelbinder
Recorded in November 2019 at Porgy & Bess, Vienna by Markus Wallner
Mixed by Markus Wallner
Mastered by Vlado Dzihan
Video by Manuel Pitsch

Released on Traumton Records
www.shakestew.com

----

Liner Notes from (A)live!:

We present this album at a most challenging point in time, when we are all feeling the loss of social interaction in many aspects of life. 
We are deeply convinced that everything we create and play is meant to exist in relation with the presence of other people, who influence the direction the music takes. The spirit behind this album is to emphasize the existential need for our music to be performed live, and the fact that it can only live and thrive as a collective phenomenon in an organic exchange with the audience. The emotions and sounds created in these moments are never private. Each and every person in the audience contributes to the process, in a constant interaction with the musicians. Therefore, we cannot simply execute our music on stage; we strive, with the help of everyone present, to bring it to life. Time and time again. This is why we play.

Seit 1998 arbeitet das Ensemble DRA an einer spezifischen Form zeitgen├Čssischer Musik mit hohen Improvisationsanteilen. Hinter DRA verbergen sich die Initialen der drei Musiker Christopher Dell (Vibraphon), Christian Ramond (Bass) und Felix Astor (Schlagzeug), DRA steht aber auch f├╝r ŌĆ×dynamic-related actionŌĆ£. Und mit Dynamik haben die Aktionen des Trios wahrlich viel zu tun, das musikalische Geschehen pulsiert ohne Unterlass. Bei ihrem Konzert in Bonn stellte die Band ihr drittes Album vor, 3rd Critique, mit dem sie ihre Forschungen am rhythmisch und harmonisch Machbaren auf eine neue Ebene hebt.

videos.title: Bill Frisell Trio | Jazz | Guitar
videos.description:
­¤Öī Welcome to VictorŌĆÖs Places! In this video weŌĆÖll listen to an American jazz guitarist who has won numerous awards over the course of his career.

Over the years, William Richard Frisell has contributed to the work of such collaborators as Paul Motian, John Zorn, Elvis Costello, Ginger Baker, The Los Angeles Philharmonic, Suzanne Vega, Loudon Wainwright III, Van Dyke Parks, Vic Chesnutt, Rickie, Lee Jones, Ron Sexsmith, Vinicius Cantu├Īria, Marc Johnson (in "Bass Desires"), Ronald Shannon Jackson and Melvin Gibbs (in "Power Tools"), Marianne Faithful, John Scofield, Jan Garbarek, Lyle Mays, Vernon Reid, Julius Hemphill, Paul Bley, Wayne Horvitz, Hal Willner, Robin Holcomb, Rinde Eckert, The Frankfurt Ballet, film director Gus Van Sant, David Sanborn, David Sylvian, Petra Haden and numerous others, including Bono, Brian Eno, Jon Hassell and Daniel Lanois on the soundtrack for Wim Wenders' film Million Dollar Hotel.

This work has established Frisell as one of the most sought-after guitar voices in contemporary music. The breadth of such performing and recording situations is a testament not only to his singular guitar conception, but his musical versatility as well. This, however, is old news by now. In recent years, it is Frisell's role as composer and band leader which has garnered him increasing notoriety.

"For over ten years Bill Frisell has quietly been the most brilliant and unique voice to come along in jazz guitar since Wes Montgomery. In light of this, it may be easy to overlook the fact that he may also be one of the most promising composers of American music on the current scene". - Stereophile

"Bill Frisell is the Clark Kent of the electric guitar. Soft-spoken and self-effacing in conversation, he apparently breathes in lungfuls of raw fire when he straps on his (guitar)...His music is not what is typically called jazz, though it turns on improvisation; it's not rock'n roll; and it sure ain't that tired dinosaur called fusion. In one of the biggest leaps of imagination since the Yardbirds and Jimi Hendrix, Frisell coaxes and slams his hovering split-toned ax into shapes of things to come...But besides being a guitar genius, he's turned into a terrific songwriter. Like Monk, Frisell's harmonic and melodic ideas form a succinct, seamless mesh with outer sonic and rhythmic ideas about his ax". - Spin

"Frisell just has a knack for coaxing the most inviting sounds out of the instrument, and the composition skills to put them in just the right order. Combine a Colorado youth given to soul and C&W with solid jazz training, abetted by a decade-long residency in the heart of NYC's avant scene, multiplied by a fun factor of X (he has scored Buster Keaton's films) and you've got a recipe damn near perfection". - The Mirror

Subscribe now and become part of our community of Jazz loverŌĆÖs! ­¤ÄĄ­¤Æ¢

Bill Frisell Trio | Jazz | Guitar

Rudy Royston - drums 
Thomas Morgan - bass

videos.title: CLARA HABERKAMP TRIO live @ JazzHall Hamburg
videos.description:
"Clara Haberkamp is making waves on the German jazz scene, featuring Norwegian drummer Jarle Vespestad alongside Eva Kruse on bass. The set fizzes with energy,  invention and emotion.  The material is a mixture of HaberkampŌĆÖs own writing and some intriguing choices.
Tightly woven patterns become subtly different with repetition and gradually break up, piano and bass seeming to float around each other, jostled by cross-rhythms from VespestadŌĆÖs drums.
The resulting music is, in the spirit of great trios, an absorbing conversation between top-drawer musicians. "

CLARA HABERKAMP TRIO - live @ JazzHall Hamburg | 2026

videos.description:
Since its founding in 2010, the Melt Trio has emerged as one of the worldŌĆÖs most innovative guitar trios in contemporary jazz. With their progressive and unique sound, they captivate critics, music lovers, and concertgoers at jazz festivals and club gigs around the globeŌĆöthey have already been nominated for the ECHO JAZZ award three times in their young career. 

MELT TRIO live @ Konzerthaus, Berlin | 2026
STOP OVER 4 ŌĆō Perspectives (Part 2)

As part of the pilot phase known as ŌĆ£Zentrum Under ConstructionŌĆØ, the ŌĆ£STOP OVERŌĆØ event series has been exploring key aspects of the future institution ŌĆ×House of Jazz ŌĆō a center for jazz and improvised musicŌĆ£ since 2023. The fourth stopover on the path to establishing the ŌĆ×ZentrumŌĆ£ focused on curation: Three internationally renowned musicians have been invited to shape one evening each within this three-day series. Each artist brought their own perspective and musical experience, opening up fresh viewpoints and new spaces of sound.

videos.title: GO┼üOS X JASINSKA @ STOP OVER 4 ŌĆō Perspectives | Part 2 | Konzerthaus Berlin
videos.description:
Go┼éos x Jasinska is a musical collaboration between pianist Emilia Go┼éos and vocalist Zuza Jasinska, rooted in friendship and a shared artistic vision. Together, they create music that is intimate, fragile, and minimal, yet strikingly expressive, with a strong emphasis on emotional depth. They combine experimental approaches with song-based forms, blending the acoustic with the electronic and the tonal with the atonal. Go┼éosŌĆÖ contemporary piano techniques of improvisation meet JasinskaŌĆÖs voice, navigating between jazz, folk, singersongwriter, and experimental music. There is a certain patience in how sounds sustain and decay, vibrations overlap, and micro dynamics shiftŌĆöcreating a fragile equilibrium inviting an immersive and intimate listening experience.

The Berlin-based quartet fuse dub, bass, hip-hop, jazz, krautrock and psychedelic soundscapes into a hypnotic live experience thatŌĆÖs as raw as it is playful. Built around deep grooves, improvised textures and fearless experimentation, their music constantly mutatesŌĆötracks dissolve, reassemble and explode into new shapes. On stage LYCHEE LASSI thrives on tension and release: heavy basslines, driving beats, fractured samples and moments of sudden silence that pull the audience right to the edge. Nothing feels pre-packagedŌĆöeverything is alive, bold and unpredictable.
Unconventional, immersive and impossible to pigeonhole, Lychee Lassi donŌĆÖt just play concerts ŌĆöthey create sonic journeys. Berlin underground energy.

videos.title: LOOM & THREAD - live @ Ernst-Meier Proberaumkomplex, Leipzig | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
Loom & Thread creates music based on samples. The sampler is played as a live instrument and merges with double bass, piano and drums in a unique real-time performance. The band's second album (out early 2026 on macro) is based on saxophone and vibraphone recordings that are atomized and recontextualized through a sampler. This creates a soundscape that initially resembles acoustic instruments but quickly undergoes significant transformations due to the sharp manipulations of the sampler.

LOOM & THREAD - live @ Ernst-Meier Proberaumkomplex, Leipzig | 2025
FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: SEVDAVISION @ Cafe Engels | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Turkish/German singer, producer, lyricist and pianist Eren Solak has collaborated with highly creative people from both areas: the jazz and the electronic club scene. These two established identities form the basis for his SevdaVision.
The eminent band, highlighting Yasmin Hadisubrata on keys and Jan Burkamp on drums, is now about to set up for this inner amalgamation of a Turkish soul and German techno ŌĆō danceable, keen and honest. SevdaVision songs portray a blend of melancholic but hopeful moments of life, refined with complex but catchy melodies. Imagine James Blake calling up Chet Baker to sing over his beats while Louis Cole helps out with some beautiful harmonies. SevdaVision debut appears on Cacao Records (Panama City/Berlin).

SEVDAVISION @ Cafe Engels, Berlin | 2025
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: LUKAS K├¢NIG @ Ventil Label Night, Arkaoda | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Austrian musician Lukas Koening is primarily a drummer. But thereŌĆÖs a lot more than percussion happening, from sputtering noise to fractured circuit breaking to creepy horror-soundtrack ambience. Using electronics and pedals along with his cymbal, Koening lets his imagination fly, leading to fascinating juxtapositions between tracks and sometimes even inside them. Despite the stylistic whiplash, Koening manages to instill his music with a sense of purpose, never blasting forward blindingly or tripping over himself to find a new sound. As a result, static churn, whirring spasms and terrifying echoes are like branches of the same highly-engaging musical tree.

LUKAS K├¢NIG @ Ventil Label Night, Arkaoda Berlin | 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer
Lukas K├Čnig - drums, percussion, electronics

videos.title: ISABELLA FORCINITI @ Ventil Label Night, Arkaoda | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Isabella Forciniti, a sound artist from Italy based in Vienna, is deeply entrenched in a diverse range of artistic pursuits, embracing performance, composition, research, and innovation. Her music and live performances traverse the spectrum that stretches from meticulously crafted arrangements to impromptu improvisation. Throughout this artistic journey, she adroitly wields a toolkit of fragmented yet rhythmically captivating beats, intricate manipulations of spatial perceptions, and intriguing psychoacoustic phenomena.

videos.title: Elias Stemeseder "Lem Botanik" live @ House Of Music, Berlin | BITCHES BREW FESTIVAL
videos.description:
Elias Stemeseder' music has passion. He sounds as if he is spearing small, agile fish with his rapid fire piano notes before tapering the intensity down into a sweet, inward reverie. He uses repetition nicely, weaving in the ominous and molding unsettled segments of sound with serenity and pure beauty. What we get is a revelation of the artist's heart, mind and soul. It happens here. It is a wonderful combination of fragility, drive, and unwavering tensile strength and talent, brimming with a sense of wide-eyed and fearless adventure.

Elias Stemeseder "Lem Botanik" - live @ House Of Music, Berlin | 2021
A series by Bitches Brew Festival and @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Stefan Schultze Large Ensemble "The Buchla Suite" - Rehearsal | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The Buchla Suite: A Handcrafted Tribute to Morton Subotnick is the latest project and sound-exploration vehicle for Stefan SchultzeŌĆÖs Large Ensemble. The project was inspired by Morton SubotnickŌĆÖs seminal composition Silver Apples of the Moon, which was released in 1967on Nonesuch Records. The piece was written for the Buchla-100 synthesizer, which Subotnick designed in collaboration with engineer Don Buchla. The introduction of this instrument, and SubotnickŌĆÖs compositions which utilised it, defined what is now considered the West-Coast style of sound synthesis. His work has had a lasting impact on generations of electronic and electroacoustic musicians.

Stefan Schultze Large Ensemble "The Buchla Suite" - Rehearsal | 2022
Live from Berlin is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Kaj Duncan David - live @ Petersburg Art Space | LIVE FROM BERLIN

videos.description:
Christian K├Čgel translates the music from Queen's "Jazz" album, which is rather not Jazz, into an energetic contemporary Berlin Jazz sound - with an electric baritone guitar instead of an electric bass and two tenor saxophones in an archaic, acoustically-oriented guise. videos.title: Mario Verandi / Corinna Rosteck - live @ Kulturkirche Nikodemus, Berlin | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
In the audiovisual concert the music of Mario Verandi meets the poetic images of Corinna Rosteck. Verandi's music features delicate, impressionistic piano melodies flowing over electronic textures. Rosteck's visuals of reanimated still images and slow motion of her moving shots follow the fluid and ephemeral. Music and images merge into an immersive, kaleidoscopic audiovisual experience.

videos.title: inEvitable feat. Hayden Chisholm "When a woman drinks" @ Studio B├Črne | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Researching the balance in the coexistence of composition and improvisation, precision and authentic personal expression, contemporary music, jazz and greek folk music, greek percussionist Evi Filippou formed the quintet in.Evi.table. Greek traditional rhythms, rembetiko melodies from the ŌĆÖ20s and reharmonisations of beloved folk songs flirt with the experimental sound of BerlinŌĆÖs jazz scene. The listener enters a microcosmos which resembles at the same time a cabaret, a contemporary music concert, a greek feast and New York in the ŌĆÖ60s as Evi imagines it.

videos.title: USINE are LIVING IN A BOX #41 | live at Club Gretchen
videos.description:
The inspiration for James Banner's Usine comes via composed themes based on reworked improvisations, multi-lingual texts that are sung, spoken and screamed, as well as the ever-changing world of literature, art, and politics in the twentieth and twenty-first centuries. At the core of Usine, the golden thread of the composed runs alongside improvisational freedom, with an appreciation of both the rich history of acoustic jazz music and a recognition of the value that each individual musician and their unique geographical and cultural experience brings to the table.

videos.title: Hope and Fear by Nils Wogram MUSE Neu
videos.description:
This video features a recording studio session of Nils Wogram`s Muse Quartet at Sendesaaal Bremen. Their album Muse was awarded album of the year by the German Jazzprice, Deutscher Jazzpreis 2022. The music of the Muse Quartet is mostly calm and atmospheric and features Nils Wogram on Trombone, and composition, Kathrin Pechlof on Harp, Gareth Lubbe on Viola, Overtone singing, and Hayden Chisholm Alto Sax. This recording session is a coproduction with the Swiss Radio SRF. The video was filmed by Beat Halberschmidt from Berta Berlin. You will hear well-structured Jazz compositions with an open mind and many musical surprises. The ensemble has a unique ban sound which is captured terrifically in the historic amazing sounding Sendesaal Bremen which is considered to be one of the world's best-sounding rooms in the world. For example, pianist Alfred Brendel recorded many of his albums here. This video has no cuts or editing and gives an authentic impression of how the band sounds and what Nils Wogram`s music is about in this setting. It is quite different from other band projects of Nils Wogram and more crossover-like. 

Excerpts from a text by Wolf Kampmann
All or nothing! No half-measures! Where routine weariness becomes noticeable in the careers of many other artists, one sense that every new project trombonist Nils Wogram takes on is his very first one. 
But why start from scratch once again with a completely new band when Wogram has achieved everything a jazz musician can ever achieve with working bands such as Root 70, Nostalgia, or the Vertigo Trombone Quartet as well as alongside Aki Takase, Simon Nabatov, Bojan Z, Michel Portal, and many others? The answer is as simple as it is complex: because Nils Wogram simply canŌĆÖt help but give in to his instinctive artistic curiosity and look at what is waiting for him over the horizon, regardless of everything he has achieved,
And so everything on Muse is actually completely different than what one is used to from Nils Wogram and his musical environment. The only constant remains Hayden Chisholm, a musician who has unreservedly shared the trombonistŌĆÖs obsessions for decades and who always gives him a bit of support, not only musically but also, above all, as a human being ŌĆō despite WogramŌĆÖs willingness to take risks. 
Nils Wogram's music has always been characterized by a great sensuality. While he has celebrated the sensuality of the moment in almost all his projects so far, the sensuality of the lasting comes to the fore on Muse. Lingering in this form plays a major role in the chamber-musical feel of this constellation. The warm sounds sometimes recall the portraits of Amadeo Modigliani, images that have frozen into still lifes. To stay in the picture, the albumŌĆÖs compositions can be visualized not so much as a leaf moving jauntily in a spontaneous gust of wind, but rather as a vase that graces a room in its once-created form and plays with light. ŌĆ£There are improvised passages on Muse as well,ŌĆØ Wogram notes, ŌĆ£but they are much less influenced by jazz, a characteristic that, of course, is very much up to the protagonists. When composing, I already knew with whom I would make the music and where their strengths lay. I brought that into harmony with my musical ideas. Only the future will show the extent to which these musical forms are permanently fixed or will develop further. I donŌĆÖt know at the moment.ŌĆØ
How could you? With this CD, the Muse project is only just beginning. This laid-back openness to all the possibilities ŌĆō ones that can arise from what has just begun ŌĆō is also easily transferred to the listener. The music may be complex, but its immanent beauty and friendliness, for all its formal rigor, is also enormously relaxing for the listener. Muse is a quiet album. Every note counts. Sound is a crucial component. ŌĆ£A priority for me was to allow this quiet, sensual music to happen without questioning, from the outset, whether it would work in the form of a CD or live performance,ŌĆØ Wogram says. ŌĆ£I just like that aesthetic. But it can only work if I implement it consistently. Consistency for me is directly related to clarity. Music with a strong mood always releases strong emotions.ŌĆØ In Muse, the constellations and transformations in the compositions are as diverse as the incidence of light at different times of the day. The individual sounds interpenetrate each other with such transparency that it is not always possible to say exactly if this is a harp or viola, or where the multiphonics on the
trombone begins and where LubbeŌĆÖs overtone singing ends? ChisholmŌĆÖs saxophone runs through these sonic light shows like a spirit that continually mediates between physicality and foreboding. The harp ŌĆōa very quiet instrument on whose level all other sound sources must or may engage ŌĆō provides the timbre.

videos.title: Joe Sachse  Nils Wogram at the recording studio Freies Gero╠łll
videos.description:
Trombonist Nils Wogram and legendary former GDR guitarist Joe Sachse during their recording session to the album Freies Ger├Čll which is released on  NWOG Records. Feel the vibe of their music and get an impression of the room and sound at Studio Nord Bremen. Produced with fine vintage microphones live to two-track analog tape.  A rare cooperation of two generations of German Jazz musicians in a relaxed and authentic atmosphere. Filmed by Beat Halberschmidt from Berta Berlin with one hand camera. No editing. Pure and simple. Get as close as possible to these European Jazz artists. 
Nils Wogram - Trombone
Helmut Joe Sachse - guitar

Freies Ger├Čll [Free Scree]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
How is it possible that all of this seems so natural, friendly, joyful, and rousing ...? Probably only because two elective affinities have met here? Two musicians who grew up in completely different social environments, two fundamentally different characters, and yet two who intuitively understand each other, who admire each other, and who play together with a looseness as if it were the most natural thing in the world. Helmut ŌĆ£JoeŌĆØ Sachse, almost a quarter of a century older than Nils Wogram, grew up in the GDR and had to blaze his own trail to jazz against all the odds ŌĆō from dance and rock music to free playing and on to his own distinctive language on the instrument. Nils Wogram has grown up in an established jazz scene and made his own mark both as an instrumentalist and with a whole series of bands that have worked together over the long term. What fascinates Joe about Nils is his versatility: ŌĆ£He can play anything.ŌĆØ And Nils raves about Joe Sachse's self-sufficient musicality: ŌĆ£Very rare, very precious.ŌĆØ The two first met many years ago at the jazz festival in Clusone, Italy. Since then, Nils and Joe have repeatedly come together and give concerts.
Finally, they wanted to go into the studio together and record pieces, which then worked out in November 2021 in Bremen. The two had three days in total to work on the music and the sound in a relaxed atmosphere, and two more to record. The legendary Studio Nord in Bremen, where the musicians could also stay, provided the appropriate setting. Both had brought written pieces ŌĆō mainly for this occasion and already with the special nature of the duo in mind. The result is music that jumps off the paper and finds its own way as it tightly intertwines composed and spontaneous playing: precision and freedom in furious cooperation. Something like this only succeeds because both have invested so much in their playing that they can manage the most refined things effortlessly. Both Nils Wogram and Helmut ŌĆ£JoeŌĆØ Sachse say in unison that they feel comfortable in the flow of playing together. One notices this in the warmth of the sound, in the ease with which they find common ground, in the correspondences ŌĆō be it in rapid unison, in the with and against each other of melodic lines, in the sovereign gait through the chords and in the course over intricate rhythms. In the end, everything seems very simple, unstrained, leaving intellectual considerations behind, joyful in the best sense of the word, and affectionate towards each other and the listeners.
The musical languages find common ground and the instruments a common sound. It is surely no coincidence that Joe Sachse has always enjoyed playing with trombonists, for example in the quartet Doppelmoppel or in duos with Conny Bauer, Johannes Bauer, or Albert Mangelsdorff. And also Nils ŌĆō of whom one can certainly say that he also has something of a Mangelsdorff gene, despite all his self-determination ŌĆō plays in a duo with Conny Bauer. So there are numerous cross-connections. Not in terms of style, but in terms of a musical compass, Joe Sachse follows his spiritual mentor John McLaughlin, who once said that a musical fusion can only be convincing if it is internalized. In this way, Joe Sachse has succeeded in integrating jazz and rock, Coltrane and Hendrix, Spanish and American, acoustic and electric, harmony-oriented and free playing. None of this can be isolated today. Moreover, Joe Sachse is something of a one-man band with his own rhythm section. The way he uses his feet percussively proves to be just as complex as when he plays the strings, tapping his right foot on the guitar case as on a bass drum and his left one as on a hi-hat, both picked up by a microphone. Indeed, he has been doing this pragmatically as well as independently, his ingenuity far ahead of todayŌĆÖs high-tech. As Nils Wogram says, ŌĆ£A unique specimen.ŌĆØ
More important than technical details is this duoŌĆÖs fine sense of dynamics and dramaturgy. Nils Wogram has repeatedly emphasized how strongly he comes from the jazz tradition, despite the ways in which jazz has expanded its form of expression. Bert Noglik

The answer is as simple as it is complex: because Nils Wogram simply canŌĆÖt help but give in to his instinctive artistic curiosity and look at what is waiting for him over the horizon, regardless of everything he has achieved,
And so everything on Muse is actually completely different than what one is used to from Nils Wogram and his musical environment. The only constant remains Hayden Chisholm, a musician who has unreservedly shared the trombonistŌĆÖs obsessions for decades and who always gives him a bit of support, not only musically but also, above all, as a human being ŌĆō despite WogramŌĆÖs willingness to take risks. 
Nils Wogram's music has always been characterized by a great sensuality. While he has celebrated the sensuality of the moment in almost all his projects so far, the sensuality of the lasting comes to the fore on Muse. Lingering in this form plays a major role in the chamber-musical feel of this constellation. The warm sounds sometimes recall the portraits of Amadeo Modigliani, images that have frozen into still lifes. To stay in the picture, the albumŌĆÖs compositions can be visualized not so much as a leaf moving jauntily in a spontaneous gust of wind, but rather as a vase that graces a room in its once-created form and plays with light. ŌĆ£There are improvised passages on Muse as well,ŌĆØ Wogram notes, ŌĆ£but they are much less influenced by jazz, a characteristic that, of course, is very much up to the protagonists. When composing, I already knew with whom I would make the music and where their strengths lay. I brought that into harmony with my musical ideas. Only the future will show the extent to which these musical forms are permanently fixed or will develop further. I donŌĆÖt know at the moment.ŌĆØ
How could you? With this CD, the Muse project is only just beginning. This laid-back openness to all the possibilities ŌĆō ones that can arise from what has just begun ŌĆō is also easily transferred to the listener. The music may be complex, but its immanent beauty and friendliness, for all its formal rigor, is also enormously relaxing for the listener. Muse is a quiet album. Every note counts. Sound is a crucial component. ŌĆ£A priority for me was to allow this quiet, sensual music to happen without questioning, from the outset, whether it would work in the form of a CD or live performance,ŌĆØ Wogram says. ŌĆ£I just like that aesthetic. But it can only work if I implement it consistently. Consistency for me is directly related to clarity. Music with a strong mood always releases strong emotions.ŌĆØ In Muse, the constellations and transformations in the compositions are as diverse as the incidence of light at different times of the day. The individual sounds interpenetrate each other with such transparency that it is not always possible to say exactly if this is a harp or viola, or where the multiphonics on the
trombone begins and where LubbeŌĆÖs overtone singing ends? ChisholmŌĆÖs saxophone runs through these sonic light shows like a spirit that continually mediates between physicality and foreboding. The harp ŌĆōa very quiet instrument on whose level all other sound sources must or may engage ŌĆō provides the timbre.

videos.title: Hell of the Intellect by Nils Wogram MUSE
videos.description:
This video features a recording studio session of Nils Wogram`s Muse Quartet at Sendesaaal Bremen. Their album Muse was awarded album of the year by the German Jazzprice, Deutscher Jazzpreis 2022. The music of the Muse Quartet is mostly calm and atmospheric and features Nils Wogram on Trombone, and composition, Kathrin Pechlof on Harp, Gareth Lubbe on Viola, Overtone singing, and Hayden Chisholm Alto Sax. This recording session is a coproduction with the Swiss Radio SRF. The video was filmed by Beat Halberschmidt from Berta Berlin. You will hear well-structured Jazz compositions with an open mind and many musical surprises. The ensemble has a unique ban sound which is captured terrifically in the historic amazing sounding Sendesaal Bremen which is considered to be one of the world's best-sounding rooms in the world. For example, pianist Alfred Brendel recorded many of his albums here. This video has no cuts or editing and gives an authentic impression of how the band sounds and what Nils Wogram`s music is about in this setting. It is quite different from other band projects of Nils Wogram and more crossover-like. 

Excerpts from a text by Wolf Kampmann
All or nothing! No half-measures! Where routine weariness becomes noticeable in the careers of many other artists, one sense that every new project trombonist Nils Wogram takes on is his very first one. 
But why start from scratch once again with a completely new band when Wogram has achieved everything a jazz musician can ever achieve with working bands such as Root 70, Nostalgia, or the Vertigo Trombone Quartet as well as alongside Aki Takase, Simon Nabatov, Bojan Z, Michel Portal, and many others? The answer is as simple as it is complex: because Nils Wogram simply canŌĆÖt help but give in to his instinctive artistic curiosity and look at what is waiting for him over the horizon, regardless of everything he has achieved,
And so everything on Muse is actually completely different than what one is used to from Nils Wogram and his musical environment. The only constant remains Hayden Chisholm, a musician who has unreservedly shared the trombonistŌĆÖs obsessions for decades and who always gives him a bit of support, not only musically but also, above all, as a human being ŌĆō despite WogramŌĆÖs willingness to take risks.

ŌĆ£There are improvised passages on Muse as well,ŌĆØ Wogram notes, ŌĆ£but they are much less influenced by jazz, a characteristic that, of course, is very much up to the protagonists. When composing, I already knew with whom I would make the music and where their strengths lay. I brought that into harmony with my musical ideas. Sound is a crucial component. ŌĆ£A priority for me was to allow this quiet, sensual music to happen without questioning, from the outset, whether it would work in the form of a CD or live performance,ŌĆØ Wogram says. ŌĆ£I just like that aesthetic. But it can only work if I implement it consistently. Consistency for me is directly related to clarity. Music with a strong mood always releases strong emotions.ŌĆØ In Muse, the constellations and transformations in the compositions are as diverse as the incidence of light at different times of the day. The individual sounds interpenetrate each other with such transparency that it is not always possible to say exactly if this is a harp or viola, or where the multiphonics on the

videos.title: Root 70   Beatrice's Rituals
videos.description:
This is a video of Nils Wogram`s band Root 70 recording his song Beatrice`s Rituals during the recording Session The Pristine Sound of Root 70. Nils Wogram - Trombone, Hayden Chisholm - Alto Sax, Matt Penman - Bass, Jochen Rueckert - Drums. Recorded live to two-track analog tape at Studio Nord Bremen.  Filmed by Beat Halberschmidt, Berta Berlin
Excerpts from a text by Wolf Kampmann: "Old fashioned" or "d├®mod├®" - while this term had a dusty connotation just a few years ago, today it
expresses the opposite of being outdated, instead conveying a self-determined resistance to rapidly incoming new trends and technological developments that shape our lives but at the same time detach us from them because we always have to keep an eye on the next new thing. "The Pristine Sound Of Root 70," the new album by Nils Wogram's band Root 70, is, in the best sense of the word, old-fashioned in a very lively, refreshing, and authentic way. It allows us to pause, reflect, breathe, and decelerate. From the first note, it offers something familiar that only wants to be rediscovered and adapted. If you want to refer to the past, the album sounds like a meeting of the Gerry Mulligan Bob Brookmeyer Quartet with the Albert Mangelsdorff Quartet of the late 1960s. Trombonist Nils
Wogram, saxophonist Hayden Chisholm, bassist Matt Penman, and drummer Jochen Rueckert manage to open entirely new doors and windows for our time with the combative serenity of those years.
"I wanted to create something timeless," Wogram emphasizes. "I do have an affinity for the old-fashioned. The challenge is that it shouldn't be stale. I wanted to completely free myself from a specific zeitgeist or directed concept. As you get older, and as a band ages, it becomes
increasingly difficult to continuously find something new within the possibilities of a particular instrumentation and personnel composition. The framework is predetermined. But you can feature
and celebrate the existing peculiarities and nuances."......
......Last but not least, "The Pristine Sound Of Root 70" is a product of its time because due to the involuntary hiatus of the global lockdown, Wogram, Chisholm, Penman, and Rueckert were thrown back onto themselves. Freed from the incessant touring routine, they individually and collectively
had the leisure to put all their values to the test. Suddenly, personal memory becomes a driving force of the musical creative process again. Root 70 has no fear of the famous and infamous guilty pleasures and honestly allows what happens in and with them.

videos.title: The Killing Popes - live @ House of Music, Berlin | BITCHES BREW FESTIVAL
videos.description:
Disjointed structures, jumping grooves, a jazz statement for the second decade of the 21st century, introjecting aspects of art rock and electronic club music. ItŌĆÖs funny, itŌĆÖs mind blowing, itŌĆÖs still new and different, itŌĆÖs the real equivalent of these so weird present times, but also an invitation to go ahead and party again, even if any body contortions you make with it hardly can be called dance. The old new jazz doesnŌĆÖt have to die to give place to the new new one.

The Killing Popes - live @ House of Music, Berlin | 2021
A series by Bitches Brew Festival and @berta.berlin.
